channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: America’s ‘Laser Dome’ starts here
item.description: The U.S.military is paving the way for the regular deployment of high-energy laser weapons on American soil for air defense amid the expanding threat of low-cost weaponized drones. The Federal Aviation Administration and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Jared Keller, Fast Company

item.title: How to hide your sensitive info (for real) when using ChatGPT and other AI chatbots
item.description: Like many, I’ve never met a chatbot I trust completely. Not only do they have a propensity to hallucinate by making up facts, but you can never be sure what their parent companies do with the information you provide.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Flawed Cisco update threatens to stop APs from getting further patches
item.description: Cisco admins are scrambling to patch a critical flash memory overflow vulnerability in over 200 Cisco Systems IOS XE-based models of wireless access points (APs), caused by a recent flawed software update.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 20 tricks for more efficient Android messaging
item.description: No matter what type of Android phone you carry or how you usually use it, one thing is a near-universal constant: You’re gonna spend a ton of time messing with messages. The messages may be from clients, colleagues, or your cousin Crissy from Cleveland (damn it, Crissy!).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Exciting Python features are on the way
item.description: Transformative new Python features are coming in Python 3.15. In addition to lazy imports and an immutable frozendict type, the new Python release will deliver significant improvements to the native JIT compiler and introduce a more explicit agenda for how Python will support WebAssembly.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Lawmakers gathered quietly to talk about AI. Angst and fears of 'destruction' followed
item.description: Lawmakers on Capitol Hill conducted a roundtable with leading AI executives and academics to discuss the potential transformative impacts of the technology on American society.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Artemis II astronauts praise their moonship's performance, especially the heat shield
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts who ignited a lunar renaissance are giving high marks to their moonship for its performance during reentry — especially the heat shield.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The two-pass compiler is back – this time, it’s fixing AI code generation
item.description: If you came up building software in the 1990s or early 2000s, you remember the visceral satisfaction of determinism. You wrote code. The compiler analyzed it, optimized it, and emitted precisely the machine instructions you expected. Same input, same output. Every single time.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Blancco confirms Mac adoption is accelerating
item.description: While sales of new Macs are surging the second-user market is also seeing strong momentum, prompting Blancco Technology Group and Cambrionix to introduce a new solution to help quickly erase and prepare large numbers of Macs for sale.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Linux 7.0 debuts with some big changes for networking
item.description: The Linux 7.0 kernel is now out, and it’s one of the most impactful releases in years for networking professionals. The Linux kernel is the core of a Linux operating system distribution. Linux is commonly used as a foundation for operating systems in the cloud and as a base for networking.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI is now powering cyberattacks, Microsoft warns
item.description: Microsoft Threat Intelligence says cybercriminals are using AI across nearly every stage of a cyberattack to move faster and scale their operations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hands-on with the Google Agent Development Kit
item.description: The Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) is a flexible and modular open-source framework for developing and deploying AI agents. It is optimized for Gemini and the Google ecosystem, but the ADK is model-agnostic, deployment-agnostic, and built for compatibility with other frameworks.
item.dc:creator: Info World
